name: Abacus Global Management, Inc.
business: Abacus Global Management is a financial services company specializing in alternative asset management, data-driven wealth solutions, technology innovations, and institutional services. It operates through four divisions: Life Solutions (purchasing and managing life insurance policies), Asset Management (managing alternative investment funds and ETFs), Technology Services (mortality verification and data analytics), and Wealth Advisors. The company sells its services to consumers, financial advisors, institutional investors, pension funds, government agencies, and insurance-related businesses.

exposures.sentence: Abacus Global Management (NYSE: ABX) is a leading financial services company specializing in alternative asset management, data-driven wealth solutions, technology innovations, and institutional services.

The Company, through ABL Intel, uses its proprietary technology based on health and longevity data sets to provide services to pension funds, government agencies, insurance-related businesses, as well as other entities that benefit from real-time mortality verification, missing participant verification, and other services specific to the life insurance market.

catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, the Company launched an asset-based finance (ABF) strategy within Abacus Asset Group, targeting the $20 trillion-plus asset-based finance market.

catalysts.event: Board approval of annual cash dividend and share repurchase program
catalysts.sentence: In November 2025, the Board of Directors approved an annual cash dividend of $0.20 per share and a $10 million share repurchase program, reflecting confidence in the Company’s long-term business model, recurring earnings, and capital strength.

catalysts.event: Completion of $50 million investment-grade securitized note backed by life insurance assets
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, the Company completed a $50 million investment-grade securitized note backed by life insurance assets, establishing a repeatable, lower-cost funding mechanism while retaining servicing rights and generating ongoing fee-based revenue.
On October 6, 2025, we completed the acquisition of AccuQuote, an online life insurance brokerage company that offers customers quotes from multiple insurance providers through a single platform.

catalysts.event: Addition to Russell 2000 and Russell 3000 Indexes
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, the Company was added to the Russell 2000 and Russell 3000 Indexes, broadening our visibility with institutional investors.

catalysts.event: Name change to Abacus Global Management, Inc.
catalysts.sentence: On February 27, 2025, we changed our name to Abacus Global Management, Inc.

tamClaims.claim: Total face value of U.S. life insurance policies in force is approximately $14 trillion.
tamClaims.sentence: The face value of all in force U.S. life insurance industry policies is approximately $14 trillion.

tamClaims.claim: Annual life settlements market opportunity is $224 billion according to a 2025 Conning Strategic Study.
tamClaims.sentence: Specifically, the scale of the life settlements market opportunity is $224 billion each year, according to the 2025 A Conning Strategic Study.

tamClaims.claim: In 2024, the life settlements industry captured only approximately 2% of the annual market of lapsed life insurance policies.
tamClaims.sentence: However, in 2024, the life settlements industry captured only approximately 2% of the annual market of lapsed life insurance policies.

tamClaims.claim: Total face value of life insurance policies is expected to grow from approximately $6 billion in 2022 to approximately $8 billion in 2028, a 5% CAGR.
tamClaims.sentence: The total face value of life insurance policies is expected to grow from approximately $6 billion in 2022 to approximately $8 billion in 2028, which is a 5% compounded annual growth rate.

tamClaims.claim: The asset-based finance market is estimated at more than $20 trillion.
tamClaims.sentence: In January 2026, the Company launched an asset-based finance (ABF) strategy within Abacus Asset Group, targeting the $20 trillion-plus asset-based finance market.
